symbols: desert setting — ascetic withdrawal and encounter with God
skull — memento mori and contemplation of mortality
description: A dark, atmospheric painting by Salvator Rosa showing Saint Jerome as a solitary penitent figure in a rugged desert landscape, rendered in brown and earth tones with dramatic lighting.
theological_description: Jerome's desert retreat follows the biblical pattern of wilderness preparation — Moses, Elijah, and Jesus himself all encountered God in the desert. Jerome devoted his life to Scripture translation, embodying his principle: 'Ignorance of Scripture is ignorance of Christ.' The desert setting recalls Jesus's temptation: 'Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that comes from the mouth of God' (MAT.4.4).

scripture_refs.reason: Jesus in the wilderness, the pattern Jerome follows
O God, you are my God, earnestly I seek you in a dry and weary land
